description: WO-ML-05F code-switching eval. Tests Lori's behavior when narrators mix English + Spanish in a single turn or alternate languages across turns. Real bilingual narrators (Hispanic/Latino US elders, second-generation immigrants, Spanish-speaking caregivers) routinely code-switch — sometimes mid-sentence, sometimes by topic, sometimes by emotional register. The mirror rule (LANGUAGE MIRRORING RULE in prompt_composer) says Lori should answer in the dominant language of the most recent turn, NOT translate or correct the narrator's language choice. These cases probe that behavior + downstream guards (Spanish perspective, fragment, name extraction, phantom-noun, correction-ack).

cases.user_text: Hola, me llamo María. I just moved here from Lima, Peru.
cases.comment: Code-switched intro. Name should capture as 'María' (accent preserved). Lori may answer in either language — both are valid mirrors of the most recent dominant span. Expected to NOT auto-translate.

cases.user_text: I came to this country in 1972. Mi mamá nunca aprendió inglés.
cases.comment: Narrator shifts to Spanish for the emotionally-loaded family detail. Lori should mirror the Spanish span. The 'mi mamá' phrase MUST trigger the perspective guard — Lori must not echo 'Mi mamá' as if it's hers.

cases.user_text: no, tengo dos hijos, no tres
cases.lori_prior_turn: It sounds like you raised three children together.
cases.comment: Lori speaks English; narrator corrects in Spanish. The compose_correction_ack composer must emit the ack in Spanish ('Lo entiendo — dos hijos...') so the narrator hears their correction respected in their own language.

cases.user_text: no, nací en Lima, no en Cuzco
cases.comment: Pure Spanish birthplace correction. Compose ack in Spanish.
Spanish 'quería decir X, no Y' pattern. Composer ack must be Spanish.

cases.user_text: Cuando mi abuela hablaba de Perú, decía que extrañaba las montañas, el mercado donde compraba maíz, y el sonido de las campanas por la mañana.
cases.comment: Narrative-connection context. Extractor must NOT route 'Perú' to grandparents.birthPlace (BUG-ML-SHADOW-EXTRACT-PLACE-AS-BIRTHPLACE-01). Lori must mirror Spanish AND repair perspective ('Mi abuela' → 'Tu abuela' if she echoes). Should NOT yes/no-close ('¿verdad?').

cases.user_text: My grandmother — mi abuela — used to make tortillas every Sunday.
cases.comment: Bilingual narrators sometimes use both kinship terms in the same sentence as a translation aid for themselves. Lori should NOT echo 'Mi abuela' (perspective guard) and should NOT correct the code-switch. The english-leading version 'My grandmother' is the dominant subject; Spanish 'mi abuela' is a translation gloss.

cases.user_text: Mi mamá tomaba mi mano cuando teníamos miedo.
cases.comment: STT may mishear 'mi mano' / 'mi miedo' as a proper noun. Phantom-noun guard must not let Lori invent a person who isn't there. Note: Whisper accent-strip variants of common Spanish words must not become proper-noun candidates.

Spanish narrator confirms Lori's prior question. No correction parsing should fire. Composer should treat as a confirmation, not a no-parse fallback. This is a regression check that 'sí' alone doesn't trip Spanish detection + correction-ack pipeline.

cases.user_text: I worked in la fábrica for thirty years and tuve dos hijos.
cases.comment: Heavy code-switching: English verb + Spanish noun + English count + Spanish verb + Spanish count. Phase 5B parser SHOULD catch 'tuve dos hijos' even with English context surrounding it. Composer ack should be Spanish (looks_spanish detects function words).

cases.user_text: Mi abuelo siempre contaba esas historias de cuando era joven en el campo.
cases.comment: Narrator tells a story; Lori's question MUST close cleanly with '?', NOT trail on a dangling demonstrative ('esas.' / 'esos.'). BUG-ML-LORI-SPANISH-FRAGMENT-REPAIR-02 closes with '?' when ¿ is unclosed. Verify the live output is well-formed Spanish.

cases.user_text: Hola Lori, soy Carmen Núñez. Tengo setenta y dos años. Vivo en Albuquerque.
cases.comment: Pure Spanish multi-detail intro. Name capture must preserve accent ('Núñez' not 'Nunez'). Lori should respond in Spanish. Numbers in Spanish words ('setenta y dos años') MUST NOT be parsed as a child count.
